description: Adversarial FR+EN red-team corpus for the local PII firewall (#104). Each case is text a bilingual developer might dictate; block-tier PII is planted to test LEAKAGE (must_redact spans that must NOT survive redaction) and clean tokens are planted to test OVER-REDACTION (must_keep spans that MUST survive). Hard negatives — non-Luhn card numbers, bad-checksum IBANs, git SHAs, code symbols, Scunthorpe words — are the precision tests: the firewall must leave them alone, because a wrong autocorrect is worse than a visible mishear. The `denylist` is loaded once and applied to every case. Credential-shaped tokens are written as `{{AWS}}`/`{{GHP}}`/`{{STRIPE}}` markers and assembled at load time (tests/test_pii_eval.py), so no real-looking secret sits in the tracked file and GitHub's own scanner stays happy. Grow it from every real near-miss.
